pr.title: Splits all the remaining character sections up to their large .o chunk
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Large mechanical assembly-splitting PR that moved the remaining character-related text from monolithic asm/text_3.s and asm/text_4.s into per-character asm files, plus a shared asm/ftbosslib.s chunk for Master Hand and Crazy Hand code. obj_files.mk was updated to build the new object files. The PR body states that Donkey Kong was temporarily split into ftdonkey_1 and ftdonkey_2 because one DK attack was located before Mario in link/order terms, with intent to later replace this with ft[Name]Special* move-based naming once moves were identified.
what_changed: Removed most of the remaining character assembly from asm/text_3.s, with 58,182 deleted lines and only 6 added lines.
Removed the corresponding remaining block from asm/text_4.s, deleting 21,198 lines.

Created many per-character assembly files under asm/, including ftkirby.s, ftmasterhand.s, ftcrazyhand.s, ftfox.s, ftgamewatch.s, ftseak.s, ftlink.s, ftfalcon.s, ftdonkey_1.s, ftdonkey_2.s, ftmario.s, ftpichu.s, ftgigakoopa.s, ftfalco.s, ftemblem.s, ftganon.s, ftsandbag.s, ftzakoboy.s, and ftzakogirl.s.
Added asm/ftbosslib.s as a shared boss-library split for code common to Master Hand and Crazy Hand.
Updated obj_files.mk with 20 added lines so the newly split assembly object files participate in the build.

Preserved raw assembly labels and function placeholders such as func_8015BC88, func_8015BD20, lbl_8015BCE4, and SDA labels in the split files; the diff excerpt shows no C decompilation or semantic renaming in this PR.
smart_moves: Split by character-oriented object boundaries rather than attempting semantic decompilation at the same time, reducing risk in a very large mechanical change.
Isolated ftbosslib as a shared Master Hand/Crazy Hand block because the author identified it as the first asserted file name after a large block of non-asserted character functions.
Documented the temporary Donkey Kong split naming decision in the PR body, including why ftdonkey_1 and ftdonkey_2 were used and what future naming should replace them.
Updated obj_files.mk in the same PR as the assembly file split, keeping source layout and build inputs synchronized.
decomp_lessons: For early-stage decomp work, large monolithic text sections can be made more searchable and tractable by carving them into file-sized or character-sized assembly chunks before matching C is available.
Asserted file names are useful anchors for object-boundary discovery, especially after long non-asserted regions.
When a split boundary is uncertain, use explicit temporary names and record the uncertainty in the PR so future contributors know what should be refined.
Avoid mixing large assembly relocation work with semantic renaming or C conversion unless necessary; this PR appears intentionally mechanical.
Shared code that belongs to multiple actors, such as Master Hand and Crazy Hand, may deserve a neutral shared file name rather than being assigned to one character.
naming_conventions: Per-character assembly files use lowercase ft-prefixed names such as ftmario.s, ftfox.s, ftkirby.s, ftfalcon.s, and ftlink.s.
Boss-related shared code was named ftbosslib.s, described as shared by Master Hand and Crazy Hand.
Donkey Kong was temporarily split into ftdonkey_1.s and ftdonkey_2.s because ordering did not cleanly fit the character list.
The PR body mentions future desired move-specific naming in the form ft[Name]Special* once individual moves are identified.
The file asm/ftseak.s preserves the project spelling present in the changed file list rather than normalizing it to another character spelling.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Moved raw .text assembly into separate files while retaining .include "macros.inc" and .section .text directives.
Kept global function labels and branch labels intact during the split to preserve linkability and address correspondence.
Used object-file-level build integration through obj_files.mk rather than leaving the new asm files as orphaned slices.
Relied on ordering and asserted file-name evidence to determine split points where full symbol identification was not yet available.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or reviews were present in the supplied PR context.
No explicit reviewer feedback is available for this PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that replace ftdonkey_1.s or ftdonkey_2.s with move-specific ftDonkeySpecial-style names.
Find subsequent PRs that decompile or rename functions originally split into asm/ftbosslib.s.
Search for later cleanup of asm/ftseak.s naming or migration into character-specific C files.
Compare obj_files.mk ordering after PR 46 with later character split or decomp PRs to see how object boundaries evolved.
